hero.subtitle: Authorization Code Exchange for web and mobile — Google setup, API reference, and frontend examples.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Google login is optional and controlled by GOOGLE_AUTH_ENABLED. When disabled, Google endpoints return 503 Service Unavailable. JWTs are never placed in the redirect URL; the API issues a one-time exchangeCode (Redis, 60 seconds).
items: Same token response shape as POST /auth/login

console.title: Google Cloud Console
console.description: Create (or edit) an OAuth 2.0 Client ID of type Web application. The authorized redirect URI must match GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL exactly.

credentials.title: 1. Open credentials
steps: Go to Google Cloud Console and select your project (top bar)
In the left menu: APIs & Services → Credentials
Edit an existing OAuth client (pencil icon), or Create Credentials → OAuth client ID (Application type: Web application)

urls.title: 2. Configure URLs
urls.description: On the Web application settings page, fill the link sections as follows (use your API host and port).

jsOrigins.title: Authorized JavaScript origins
jsOrigins.description: Base URL of this Nest API, including the port — no path.

redirects.title: Authorized redirect URIs
redirects.description: Exact callback route on this API (same value as GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL). Do not use your SPA path here.

urls.note: After saving, copy Client ID and Client Secret into your .env. Frontend post-login URLs belong in GOOGLE_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST, not in Google’s redirect URI list.

env.title: Environment variables
env.description: Loaded from .env via src/config/google-oauth.config.ts (namespace googleOAuth on ConfigService). See .env.example.

roles.title: Default roles for new Google users
roles.description: New Google users receive roles: [] by default (same as password register). Set GOOGLE_OAUTH_DEFAULT_ROLES in .env to a comma-separated list of Role values (e.g. user or user,manager). Existing users linked by email keep their current roles.

flow.title: Sequence
items: User consents on Google; browser hits GET /api/v1/auth/google/callback
API upserts user, stores exchangeCode in Redis, redirects to redirect?code=...
Client POSTs { code } to /api/v1/auth/exchange and receives JWTs

start.title: 1. Start OAuth
start.description: Opens Google consent. Query redirect must match a prefix in GOOGLE_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST (or omit to use the first allowlist entry).

callback.title: 2. Callback
callback.description: Handled by the API. Redirects to your app with ?code= (or &code= if the URL already has a query).

exchange.title: 3. Exchange code
exchange.description: Public endpoint. Consumes the code (single use) and returns the same shape as login.

web.title: Frontend — Web (SPA)
web.description: Typical flow: button starts OAuth in the same window (or popup); a callback route on your frontend origin reads code from the query string and calls exchange.
notes: Add your frontend callback URL to GOOGLE_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST (e.g. http://localhost:5173/auth/callback)
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL must point at the Nest API, not the SPA
Store accessToken / refreshToken securely (memory + httpOnly cookie patterns as you prefer)

mobile.title: Frontend — Mobile (deep link)
mobile.description: Use a custom scheme (or universal link) in the allowlist. After Google, the API redirects to myapp://success?code=.... The app opens, parses the code, and calls exchange.
notes: Register the scheme in the OS / Expo app.json
Exchange over HTTPS against your API base URL

security.title: Security notes
items: Redirect URLs must match GOOGLE_REDIRECT_ALLOWLIST prefixes
Exchange codes are single-use and expire in 60 seconds (Redis)
Existing local users are linked by verified Google email (googleId set; roles unchanged)
Google-only users have password null — password login / change-password will fail until a password is set

userModel.title: User model
userModel.description: Postgres entity / interface fields used by Google login:
items: googleId — unique nullable Google subject ID
isActive — set true for new Google users (email verified by Google)

samples.fullPageRedirect: // Full-page redirect to Nest → Google → back to SPA
samples.persistTokens: // persist tokens as your app requires
